die: verb. To stop living; to become dead; to undergo death. followed by of as an indication of direct cause; general use:
diene: noun. An organic compound, especially a hydrocarbon, containing two double bonds.

ice: symbol. ISO 639-2 /B language code for Icelandic.
nee: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Kumak.
need: noun. A requirement for something; something needed.
niece: noun. A daughter of one’s sibling, brother-in-law, or sister-in-law; either the daughter of one's brother (" fraternal niece "), or of
vee: noun. The name of the Latin script letter V / v.
vein: noun. A blood vessel that transports blood from the capillaries back to the heart.
veined: adjective. Having veins or veinlike markings.
vend: verb. Synonym of sell, to sell through a vending machine.
vendee: noun. The person to whom something is sold; a purchaser.
vice: noun. Bad or immoral behaviour. (Especially often, a habit that harms oneself or others)
vide: verb. Clipping of divide (to split or separate into two or more parts.).
